product_updates.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
product_updates.content: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

product_updates.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
product_updates.content: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

product_updates.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
product_updates.content: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

product_updates.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
product_updates.content: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

product_updates.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
product_updates.content: OpenAI's agentic coding tool has gotten a major makeover, with a variety of new powers and abilities.

product_updates.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
product_updates.content: Krieger's departure and any forthcoming design tools will be another data point for investors who fear the SaaSpocalypse — that the largest AI labs will come to dominate software businesses, a thesis that has rocked public markets at times this year.

product_updates.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
product_updates.content: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with AI Mode.

product_updates.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
product_updates.content: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong — it's also diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is part of it.

product_updates.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
product_updates.content: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.

product_updates.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
product_updates.content: The new tools are designed to help creators throughout the entire development process.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
technical_breakthroughs.content: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. At the same time, government institutions face distinct constraints around security, governance, and operations that set them apart from their business counterparts. For this reason,…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
technical_breakthroughs.content: There’s a fault line running through enterprise AI, and it’s not the one getting the most attention. The public conversation still tracks foundation models and benchmarks—GPT versus Gemini, reasoning scores, and marginal capability gains. But in practice, the more durable advantage is structural:…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: Why having “humans in the loop” in an AI war is an illusion
technical_breakthroughs.content: The availability of artificial intelligence for use in warfare is at the center of a legal battle between Anthropic and the Pentagon. This debate has become urgent, with AI playing a bigger role than ever before in the current conflict with Iran. AI is no longer just helping humans analyze intell…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The noise we make is hurting animals. Can we learn to shut up?
technical_breakthroughs.content: When the covid-19 pandemic started, Jennifer Phillips thought about the songs of the sparrows. They were easier to hear, because the world had suddenly become quieter. Car traffic plummeted as people sheltered at home and shifted to remote work. Air travel collapsed. Cities—normally filled with t…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The quest to measure our relationship with nature
technical_breakthroughs.content: As a movement, environmentalism has been pretty misanthropic. Understandably so—we humans have done some destructive things to the ecosystems around us. In the 21st century, though, mainstream conservation is learning that humans can be a force for good. Foresters are turning to Indigenous burnin…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Is carbon removal in trouble?
technical_breakthroughs.content: Last week, news outlets reported that Microsoft was pausing carbon removal purchases. It was something of a bombshell. The thing is, Microsoft is the carbon removal market. The company has single-handedly purchased something like 80% of all contracted carbon removal. If you’re looking for someone…
